verdicts.reason: "a certain Lysene dancing girl soon became his favorite" is Gyldayn's own euphemistic phrasing (parallel to the 'found a new favorite'/'the groom's favorite' pattern), not a flat statement of a romantic bond, and no chronicler is named at this point in the text.

"if their family histories can be believed" is a mid-sentence parenthetical hedging only the immediately preceding clause (Velaryons arriving in Westeros before the Targaryens); "the oldest and closest allies of the Targaryens" is a separate, flatly-asserted sentence outside that hedge's scope.

"surely one of the most extraordinary figures of the age" is Gyldayn's own editorial praise, in an earlier, separate paragraph/sentence; the nearby "can be believed" hedge governs the Velaryons' claimed pre-Targaryen arrival, not Gyldayn's assessment of Corlys.

Flat Gyldayn narration that Baelor the Blessed ordered Mushroom's chronicle burned — a fact about Mushroom's book's fate, not a citation to his disputed Testimony content; classic false-hold (hedge term "mushroom" firing on prose about the man rather than his account).
"taking his paramour Mysaria with him" uses the explicit, non-euphemistic term "paramour" in plain Gyldayn narration; not hedged.

"his niece the princess" states a definitional genealogical fact (Daemon is the king's brother, Rhaenyra the king's daughter) that holds true independent of the disputed seduction narrative it appears inside.

The banishment itself is one of the facts every account agrees on (only the underlying scandal's particulars differ between Eustace's and Mushroom's versions) — matches the exile-is-flat calibration example directly.

The closed-door council detail (Strong urging execution) is textually anchored to Septon Eustace, who is quoted rebutting Strong in the same breath — this reads as material from Eustace's firsthand account of a private scene, not an established court-of-record fact.

"the groom's favorite" is the euphemism-class insinuation named explicitly in the calibration guidance; this is plain Gyldayn narration listing tourney competitors, with no chronicler attribution nearby.

Flat narration of a public tourney injury/death witnessed by hundreds; the adjacent "Mushroom tells us" attaches to the following sentence (Laenor's bedside vigil), not to the cracked-helm/death claim.
Flat narration of a public joust injury; the parenthetical Mushroom reference supplies only the nickname "Brokenbones," not a hedge on the injury itself.
Flat statement of Cole's known political alignment with the queen's party; no hedge or attribution governs this clause.

"It was said that" hedges only the preceding clause about Alicent's private reaction to Joffrey's death; the request that Cole be made her protector is a separate, flatly-stated independent clause describing a public appointment.
"ever at Rhaenyra's side at feast and ball and hunt" describes Harwin's public court-favorite role (foremost of the blacks), not a romantic euphemism, and is not hedged.

"found a new favorite" is the exact euphemism-class pattern named in the calibration guidance; this introductory sentence carries no chronicler attribution (the later Mushroom material about the threesome is a separate, later claim).

verdicts.who: Daemon exiled after the Rhaenyra scandal
verdicts.reason: Matches the calibration's explicit example: the fact of exile is agreed by every account even though the underlying scandal's details differ between chroniclers.

verdicts.who: Death of Joffrey Lonmouth at the wedding tourney
verdicts.reason: Matches the calibration's explicit example: his death six days after the tourney is undisputed public fact, not part of the disputed bedchamber material.

The reconciliation is a witnessed public event and "a scandal over Rhaenyra" is deliberately non-specific about which disputed version (Eustace's vs. Mushroom's) is true, so the summary doesn't take a side; both underlying facts (reconciliation, exile) are undisputed.

Flat biographical description (age, having read to the dying Jaehaerys) restated from earlier in the text; the disputed insinuation about her virtue/relationship with Viserys before Aemma's death is a separate sentence a few lines later, explicitly flagged as "calumnies... never proved, though Mushroom repeats them."
Restates the same flat, undisputed public-role fact as the COMPANION_OF edge above (foremost of the blacks at Rhaenyra's side).
Same undisputed public tourney-death fact cited in the calibration guidance; not sourced to Mushroom specifically.
